key_insight: Synchrotron X-ray diffraction found no evidence for the proposed lower-symmetry Pn2_1a structural distortion in Ca3(Ru,Ti)2O7, indicating that the reported altermagnetic behavior is likely electronic rather than lattice-driven.
engineering_relevance: This work sets a metrology benchmark for detecting sub-picometer structural changes in correlated oxides and helps distinguish true symmetry lowering from purely electronic phase transitions in quantum materials.
method: Targeted single-crystal synchrotron X-ray diffraction on Ca3Ru2O7 and Ca3(Ru0.99Ti0.01)2O7
Search for diffraction signatures of the predicted Pn2_1a phase down to 20 K
Compare experimental displacement sensitivity (~60–200 fm) against DFT-predicted distortions (~1 pm)
evidence_signal: No Pn2_1a diffraction signature was detected within the experimental sensitivity limit, despite the predicted ~1 pm distortion from DFT.
watch_item: The absence of a detectable lattice distortion does not fully exclude extremely small or dynamically fluctuating symmetry breaking below the measurement limit.
paper_title: Evaluating the Structural Basis for Polar Altermagnet Candidate Ca$_{3}$(Ru,Ti)$_{2}$O$_{7}$
